4.reason: [The stack is declared as char array, but it should store integers. This causes incorrect storage and output of integer values.]

15.reason: [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since top is zero-based and MAX is 100. Checking top == 100 allows overflow (index 100 is out of bounds).]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since top is zero-based and MAX is 100. Checking top == 100 allows overflow (index 100 is out of bounds).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since stack indices go from 0 to 99 for size 100. Checking top == 100 allows pushing beyond capacity.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since top starts at -1 and valid indices are 0 to 99. Comparing with 100 allows overflow.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since the stack indices go from 0 to 99 for size 100. Comparing with 100 leads to buffer overflow.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [The condition should be top == MAX - 1 or top == 99, since stack indices go from 0 to 99 for size 100. top==100 means stack overflow has already occurred.]
